criteria.label: State the role of train, validation, and test sets in one workflow.
criteria.example: Write three bullets: train → fit θ; validation → pick hyperparameters; test → one final generalization score after selection.

criteria.label: Explain why hyperparameter search must not use test metrics.
criteria.example: One sentence: repeated test peeking makes the test set part of model selection, so the final test score is optimistically biased.

criteria.label: Produce a 70/15/15 stratified three-way split in sklearn.
criteria.example: Show two train_test_split calls with test_size=0.3 then 0.5 on the temp fold, both with stratify=y, printing len(y_train), len(y_val), len(y_test).

criteria.label: Choose the correct split strategy for a given scenario.
criteria.example: Given user click logs with repeated users: recommend group split by user_id, not random row shuffle, because the same user must not appear in train and test.

criteria.label: Demonstrate leakage-free preprocessing across train, validation, and test.
criteria.example: Split first, then pipe.fit(X_train, y_train) where StandardScaler inside the pipeline learns only from X_train; score with pipe.score(X_val) and pipe.score(X_test).
